title: Federal Commandeering of State Officials
description: Congress enacts a statute requiring state executive officials to implement and enforce a federal regulatory program, in violation of Article VII Section 1.2, which prohibits commandeering state legislatures, executives, or courts. A state refuses to comply and challenges the statute. Courts void the commandeering provision. The scenario tests whether the anti-commandeering protection is judicially enforceable and whether states retain meaningful autonomy when Congress overreaches.
